title: Hyppönen — the wolverines parable (Jufo Peltomaa) + AI taking warehouse jobs
html:
If It's Smart, It's Vulnerable — Hyppönen retells startup founder Jufo Peltomaa's lecture: he opened with wolverines (territorial habits, pack sizes, our incomplete knowledge of them) — punchline: a superintelligent AI would relate to humans the way humans relate to wolverines. Mild interest, no malice, no urgency. Why wipe us out? Then pivots to AI replacing jobs — warehouse workers now wear earphones taking orders from an AI shift-supervisor. "Machines can optimize human operations better than humans, but leadership of this type is far from humane."

TL;DR

  • The wolverine parable: superintelligent AI's interest in humans would be ethological, not adversarial — extinction by indifference, not malice.
  • AI as boss, not laborer — the warehouse-via-headsets pattern means AI takes over coordination first, leaving humans as the actuators.
  • "Leadership of this type is far from humane" — the dignity cost of optimization, not the labor cost.

mikko hypponen · if its smart its vulnerable · chatgpt · ubi
